title: Hanguana minor
intro: Hanguana minor, also known as the water chestnut, is a tropical plant that belongs to the family Hanguanaceae, characterized by its unique, floating leaves and edible corms. The health benefits of Hanguana minor include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Hanguana minor is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are employed to treat conditions like fever, diarrhea, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Hanguana minor belongs to the family Hanguanaceae, which is a small family of flowering plants. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Hanguana. The classification of Hanguana minor places it as an endemic species to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant is often referred to as the 'water chestnut' or 'water caltrop', due to its unique growth habit and seed structure. Hanguana minor is closely related to the family Acoraceae, and is often grouped with other plant species that have similar characteristics.
